## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright guys, so now we're going to move on to the second phase of our testing which is Rapid Fire Round 2. Now in this round what we're doing is we're testing out different offers or different hooks if you will. We've now identified an audience and or a persona that works really well and we will have some top creative that also worked really well across those ad sets which we have pulled into a conversion objective campaign at this stage. You'll see here we've got one up and already it's clocking some sales which is really good it's got a fairly good row to begin with. Now what we want to do is we want to actually test out the offers or the hooks. So in the first round possibly you've already done this. Maybe you tested out things like free shipping or lay-by, after-pay, a discount for example. But what we want to do is now that we have our consolidated audiences that are working well as well as the top creative we want to try those hooks out on those. Now in our first round in the examples that I used we didn't really use a hook we had lay-by but it was kind of hidden in our copy. So now we want to test out well is it lay-by, is it free shipping, is it a discount, what's going to work really well. So we've just had permission from the client to test some stuff out and this is what we're going to do right now. Now what I recommend is you actually just use the top creatives that worked in your rapid fire round one and you simply just edit the headlines or the link descriptions or the copy to add that in. You can choose to create some images as well that have that hook inside of the image. Let me show you here. So for example an image like this which does have the hook that's entirely up to you but what we want to do is reduce as many variables as possible so try to avoid using brand new images or brand new products unless you're actually testing out different products for that audience unless that's what you're actually testing out. So if that's what you're doing what I would recommend is you run a few phases in this round. So the first phase is maybe you test out different products for that audience. Don't test out the hooks necessarily or use the same hook in all of those different products so that you're only testing out that one variable the product right. And then once you identify the product then you do a second phase in which you're testing out different hooks for that product. So this is what we're going to do now. Now what we don't want to be doing inside of this round I'm back inside my post engagement objective campaign and what I want to avoid doing here is actually running I'm using this audience inside of my conversion objective right now and I want to avoid running ads to this audience as well instead of post engagement because it's just going to start cannibalizing my conversion objective campaign.

## [03:00 - 05:54]

The reason for that is because Facebook will generally favor engagement based objectives or engagement based activities. So we want to make sure that we create a new ad set and what we're going to be running is ads we're going to be targeting page fans because those typically give us a good response on the CTRs and the CPCs and we can also run a second ad set targeting people that visited the website 60 days ago but excluding people that are still in our retargeting sequence so usually that's about 30 days. So let's go ahead and create those. This is also what we do in rapid fire round three we don't target these same audience. All right so with our creative we're going to build this as a single image link click so we're going to go inside our test campaign objective again. All right so you'll see here this was one of our top ads from our rapid fire test. It's currently the original ad is being run inside the conversion campaign. What we've done here is we've actually added in the hook inside of this exact same ad. This is the easiest thing to do is just use the top creative from your rapid fire test and then add the hook in or hooks if you have several and test those out against each other using the same ad format same creative as you did in your first round. So we built this in our test campaign. I'm going to go back inside of our rapid fire ads campaign here so this is stage one and I'm basically I've previewed all of those ads I'm basically going to pull them in and I'm going to be targeting page fans and the website visitors so let's go and build that now. Now don't forget to go into your funnel tracking sheet and update everything that you're doing all the creatives that are live. Also now what you need to do is now that we've set up these creatives instead of our post engagement campaign you need to make sure that they're all paused and that you send those links to the client for approval before you go live with them. Another important step guys we want to make sure that client is double checking everything. Make sure that before you send that to the client you go through that checklist which is in the SOP which is double checking grammar links the pages are all attached your UTM's your URL parameters are all installed etc etc and then send it off to the client.

## [06:03 - 08:20]

Alright so now what I've got here under this ad set is the new one for page fans. They're all all the ads under here are using post IDs from our test campaign so I can easily duplicate this and just edit out the targeting at the ad set level and they will copy the exact same ads over into our second ad set which is going to be web hits 60 days so what we're doing here I'm going to give this one a bigger budget because it should be a slightly bigger audience is we're going to create a custom audience of people that visited the website in 60 days visited site 60 days but we're going to exclude those that visited site in the last 30 days the reason for that is if someone visited the site in the last 30 days they're in our retargeting sequence so we don't want to mess with that we don't want to be targeting them with this as well. Okay so we're excluding it we're not going to be able to see the reach we're going to remove our page connection make sure this is alright and then we're going to click publish. Perfect so this is basically how to set up the second round of your rapid fire testing you're basically at this stage you have the personas the audience you're testing out different offers and different hooks so you'll see it's all set up under here with two new ad sets that you're targeting different audiences for as well because the winning ones from here were pulled into our conversion based objective and they're starting to get us sales already so we don't really want to overlap with that right now we don't want to mess with it we want to target some fresh audiences and these audiences here have already proven themselves to be interested so rather than going after a cold cold audience that we don't know we haven't validated yet it's a smart move to target a slightly warmer audience.
